run_summary: Four open positions all showing modest gains; all theses intact but no new catalysts warrant adding. Six new flags parsed: two semi-cap sector selloffs (VECO, ONTO) with no company-specific catalyst, three rational post-earnings moves (AVAV, FIZZ, BBIO) already fairly priced, one overdone sympathy play (MRCY), and one sector sympathy with mean-reversion setup (AEIS). AVAV re-entry warning applies — prior exit was on fundamental doubts about the restatement severity read and subsequent loss. New flag is confidence 3 on momentum continuation in a hot subsector with no new information vs. the exit thesis. Holding all positions; taking small exploratory stake on AEIS sector-sympathy fade as it has the cleanest mean-reversion setup and highest conviction among new names.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide semi-cap equipment selloff; no company-specific filing
entries.thesis: VECO is down sharply with no filed catalyst while peers ONTO and AEIS also crater on the same day — this looks like a sector-wide semi-cap capex re-rating (possibly triggered by a peer's guidance or a memory/foundry capex cut headline not in this feed). With SMH -4.5% and three cap-equipment names dropping double digits together, the move likely has legs beyond today because capex cycles reprice over weeks, not hours. VECO is the smallest and most levered to niche end-markets, so it tends to overshoot but continues drifting until a specific bounce catalyst appears.
entries.what_kills: VECO issues a reassuring 8-K or pre-announcement; the sector move reverses on a benign explanation (e.g., single-analyst downgrade already fully absorbed); SMH bounces sharply Monday.
entries.reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 on sector-wide semi-cap equipment selloff (no company-specific 8-K, down -23.8% in 5 days, -18.48% today on 1.34x volume). Thesis is sound: synchronized drops in VECO/ONTO/AEIS suggest a shared macro capex driver (memory or foundry guidance reset) that has 'legs' over days to weeks. However, no catalyst URL is provided, and the driver is explicitly hypothetical ('possibly triggered by...'). Cannot take a conviction-level trade on a sector move without a named catalyst. The exploratory tier (6%) would be appropriate IF the setup were sharper, but the absence of a specific source event (peer guide, customer announcement) makes this a 'wait for confirmation' scenario. Will re-flag if LRCX/AMAT guides down or memory capex cut is announced over weekend.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.thesis: Same sector catalyst chain as VECO/AEIS. ONTO is the higher-quality metrology name in the group; if the driver is a memory or HBM capex reset, ONTO's HBM/advanced-packaging exposure makes it directly in the crosshairs. Move likely continues until the specific catalyst is identified and digested.
entries.what_kills: Catalyst turns out to be a one-off peer guide with no HBM read-through; ONTO issues reassuring commentary; SMH reverses.
entries.reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 on same semi-cap sector chain as VECO, down -10.65% in 5 days, -12.46% today on 1.32x volume. Thesis (HBM capex exposure to hyperscaler cuts) is coherent and ONTO's metrology positioning makes it a direct play on advanced-packaging CapEx. However, the catalyst is 'sympathy with sector' with no named source (no catalyst_url). Same issue as VECO: the driver is inferred rather than confirmed. Will watch for a specific HBM-capex announcement (e.g., SK Hynix, Samsung, or TSMC guidance) over the weekend before committing capital.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q earnings 8-K filed 6/29 with 2.02 + 7.01 items; multi-day drone-defense momentum
entries.thesis: AVAV filed an earnings 8-K on 6/29 and has extended sharply higher over five sessions, with today's move confirming momentum. ITA +1.8% and KTOS also up — defense tape is bid and drone/loitering munitions names are the leaders. The magnitude of the multi-day run is large but supported by both a real earnings catalyst and a persistent narrative (Ukraine/defense drone demand). No mispricing edge either way at this point — the move is doing what a good earnings beat in a hot subsector should do.
entries.what_kills: A geopolitical de-escalation headline; a defense budget cut signal; the earnings quality on inspection turns out to be one-time (backlog pull-forward).
entries.reasoning: RE-ENTRY WARNING APPLIES. You closed AVAV 7 days ago at a loss (-4.75%) on the grounds that the restatement (4.02) + officer departure (5.02) setup was overestimated as a durably negative catalyst, and subsequent -7.64% 1-day realized move signaled the market was not pricing the risk underpriced. The new flag is RATIONAL conf 3 on post-earnings continuation: +39.66% in 5 days, +10.7% today, 2.1x volume, drone/defense narrative strong. While the earnings beat is real and the defense tape is genuinely bid, this is NOT new information sufficient to override the re-entry bar. The original restatement severity and officer departure questions remain unresolved; you exited because you lacked conviction on the fundamental setup, not because the price target was hit. A confidence-3 flag on momentum continuation in a hot subsector does not satisfy the 'confidence >= 4' and 'genuinely new information' dual criteria. Momentum is not new information; it is the natural consequence of a beating quarter in a strong tape. Will WATCH; if AVAV guides up on the next quarterly update or announces a major contract win, that becomes new information warranting re-entry at higher conviction.

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.thesis: Earnings 8-K filed 7/2 (items 2.02 + 8.01) two days after the print, stock +7.5% on 2.6x volume — normal post-earnings response for a low-float, low-coverage name. Move looks proportional; no obvious mispricing.
entries.what_kills: Reversal on discovery that the 8.01 item contained a negative disclosure (guidance withdrawal, litigation).
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on post-earnings pop (7.52% on 2.62x volume, earnings 8-K filed 7/2). Thesis correctly notes the move is 'proportional' and shows 'no obvious mispricing' — in other words, this is a normally-sized earnings reaction in a low-float, low-coverage name. The magnitude does not suggest under- or overpricing; it is fair value discovery. No edge exists. The 8.01 item (contained in the 8-K) is not yet known to be negative, so the 'what_kills' scenario is speculative. Skip on the basis that efficient pricing offers no edge.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst detected; likely sympathy with AVAV earnings and broader defense/ITA strength.
entries.thesis: MRCY is up double-digits with volume_multiple of only 1.07 and no 8-K, no scheduled earnings — meaning institutions are not heavily participating. The move looks like sympathy with AVAV's print plus the defense-budget narrative. Without conviction-level volume, double-digit sympathy moves in defense names tend to give back a meaningful portion within days as the news-specific tailwind to AVAV doesn't translate cleanly to MRCY's different business mix (embedded computing vs. drones).
entries.what_kills: Disclosure of an actual MRCY-specific catalyst (contract win, M&A rumor) emerges; or volume picks up materially on follow-through day signaling real institutional accumulation.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3: MRCY +10.99% on 1.07x volume with no 8-K or scheduled earnings, flagged as sympathy with AVAV defense-drone momentum. Thesis is correct that light volume on a double-digit sympathy move suggests weak institutional conviction and high fade risk. However, the setup is fragile: if volume does pick up on follow-through, the thesis breaks. The reward-to-risk on a trade sized to catch a 3-5% fade is not compelling when the thesis hinges on 'volume stays light' — a condition that can flip intraday. Skip in favor of setups with cleaner mechanical edges.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual-meeting-pattern items (5.02 director matters, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits); broader XBI/XLV strength.
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 is the classic annual meeting / director election / proxy-vote-results combination — administrative, not material. The barely-positive move on heavy volume against a strongly green XBI/XLV tape suggests the stock is mostly riding the sector rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. Magnitude appears appropriate to the catalyst (which is essentially nil at the company level).
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unexpected senior officer departure rather than routine board rotation, which would reframe the day's relative underperformance vs XBI as bearish.
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on routine governance 8-K (items 5.02/5.07/9.01 = annual meeting/director election/proxy results) coinciding with sector tailwind. Thesis correctly identifies the 8-K as administrative (nil company-level catalyst) and the stock as riding XBI/XLV strength. Magnitude is appropriate; no mispricing detected. The flag's own 'what_kills' scenario (5.02 being an unexpected officer departure) is a risk but not a base case. Skip on efficient pricing.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with SMH -3.97% sector dump (AVGO -3.67%, ORCL -2.58%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%); no AEIS-specific 8-K or news.
entries.thesis: AEIS has no 8-K, no earnings imminent, and is classified as Industrial/Electrical Equipment yet derives substantial revenue from semiconductor capital equipment customers. SMH is down nearly 4% and ONTO/VECO are down 6% — AEIS is getting tarred with the same brush despite its more diversified end-market mix (also industrial, data center, medical). On 4.9x average volume with no news, this looks like a sympathy washout that retraces faster than the pure-play semicap names once the SMH downdraft stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: A late-breaking 8-K with a customer-loss disclosure or guide-down, sector weakness extending on confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer, or AEIS-specific negative sell-side note.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The mean-reversion thesis relies on SMH stabilizing and AEIS bouncing faster than pure-plays ONTO/VECO on an unconfirmed Monday recovery, but the 5-day -6.57% move and 4.88x volume suggest capitulation may already be priced in or reflect broader fundamental repricing, not mechanical oversold. Bear case: The bull thesis rests on a binary catalyst (SMH recovery Monday) that is neither confirmed nor controllable, and the timing assumption—that a 2-3 day mean-reversion play will resolve favorably in an exploratory 6% position—conflates short-term momentum exhaustion with repricing opportunity. More critically, the 4.88x volume on a -7.21% daily move in the context of a -6.57% 5-day decline is consistent with capitulation, but capitulation does not guarantee immediate bounce; it can signal a new regime where semiconductor-adjacent names trade lower pending broader chip cycle confirmation. The claim that AEIS's diversified end-markets (data center, industrial, medical) insulate it from SMH weakness is qualitative and untested—if those end-markets are also facing growth pressure (as industrial/data-center cycles often do in tandem with semiconductor cycles), the diversification thesis is illusory. The 'asymmetric bounce' prediction (AEIS outperforms ONTO/VECO) is not anchored to any structural or operational difference articulated in the flag; it is a pattern-matching bet on a 2-3 day window with no margin for error. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on sector sympathy: AEIS -7.21% on 4.88x volume, down -6.57% over 5 days, no 8-K or earnings catalyst. Thesis is sharp: AEIS has diversified end markets (semiconductor, but also data center, industrial, medical) and is being tarred with the same brush as pure-play VECO/ONTO on SMH weakness (-3.97%). The flag explicitly predicts mean-reversion: 'AEIS bounces harder than ONTO/VECO on recovery leg' once SMH stabilizes. This is a classic oversold sympathy fade with mechanical setup: (1) no company-specific bad news, (2) heavy volume on a sector move signals capitulation, (3) portfolio diversification across end-markets creates bounce asymmetry vs. pure-plays. 4.88x volume vs. ~1.3x for VECO/ONTO is the tell that retail/forced selling has exhausted. Entering at 6% exploratory position to test the mean-reversion hypothesis over the next 2-3 trading days as SMH bounces (Friday close -3.97%; weekend/Monday open will reveal if bounce thesis holds). If SMH opens flat-to-up Monday and AEIS bounces 3-5% faster than ONTO/VECO, thesis is confirmed and adds conviction for future entry. If SMH gaps lower Monday or a negative AEIS-specific 8-K emerges, thesis breaks and position exits.)

red_team.weakest_link: The mean-reversion thesis relies on SMH stabilizing and AEIS bouncing faster than pure-plays ONTO/VECO on an unconfirmed Monday recovery, but the 5-day -6.57% move and 4.88x volume suggest capitulation may already be priced in or reflect broader fundamental repricing, not mechanical oversold.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on a binary catalyst (SMH recovery Monday) that is neither confirmed nor controllable, and the timing assumption—that a 2-3 day mean-reversion play will resolve favorably in an exploratory 6% position—conflates short-term momentum exhaustion with repricing opportunity. More critically, the 4.88x volume on a -7.21% daily move in the context of a -6.57% 5-day decline is consistent with capitulation, but capitulation does not guarantee immediate bounce; it can signal a new regime where semiconductor-adjacent names trade lower pending broader chip cycle confirmation. The claim that AEIS's diversified end-markets (data center, industrial, medical) insulate it from SMH weakness is qualitative and untested—if those end-markets are also facing growth pressure (as industrial/data-center cycles often do in tandem with semiconductor cycles), the diversification thesis is illusory. The 'asymmetric bounce' prediction (AEIS outperforms ONTO/VECO) is not anchored to any structural or operational difference articulated in the flag; it is a pattern-matching bet on a 2-3 day window with no margin for error.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: New material agreement + financial obligation 8-K on 6/30, plus sector selloff
entries.thesis: LSCC filed an 8-K on 6/30 with items 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (new financial obligation) — likely a debt raise or credit facility. Combined with the SMH -4.5% tape, the stock is down mid-single digits. A refinancing/new debt facility is usually not a material valuation event for a company of LSCC's size, so a portion of today's drop is likely sector-driven overshoot rather than filing-driven. Low confidence because the 1.01 could also be an acquisition financing (bearish if pricey) — the exhibit needs reading.
entries.what_kills: The 1.01 turns out to be an expensive acquisition, dilutive convert, or covenant-loosening amendment that signals stress; semi weakness broadens.

entries.name: Kratos Defense
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with AVAV earnings + broad defense strength
entries.thesis: KTOS is riding the same drone/defense narrative as AVAV, with no own 8-K. Five-day return ~20% suggests a lot has already been priced in, but with ITA +1.8% and defense as a Trump-friendly sector, momentum can persist. No clear mispricing to trade against.
entries.what_kills: Geopolitical thaw; a KTOS-specific negative headline; profit-taking after such a strong run.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Unknown — sector strength plus unidentified company-specific driver
entries.thesis: CRSP +8% with XBI +2.5% is roughly 3x sector beta, suggesting some company-specific catalyst exists but is not in the SEC feed (could be conference presentation, analyst note, or Casgevy commercial data leak). Without identifying the driver, I cannot commit to overdone or underdone at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify catalyst first

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no filings or earnings signal
entries.thesis: ALGN is up over 7% on 1.26x volume with no 8-K, no earnings signal, and XLV only up 0.5%. Cannot articulate a bull thesis at conf 3+ — could be a short-covering bounce, an unreported analyst upgrade, or GLP-1/obesity-drug crossread (Medicare obesity coverage headline today, but link to Invisalign is tenuous). Neither direction supportable from available evidence.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.thesis: BLDR down over 5% on 0.77x volume, no 8-K, XLI only -1%. Could be rate-sensitive weakness or peer-driven, but rates weren't dramatically moved and no specific news is visible. Cannot construct a directional call at conf 3+ without more information — the light volume argues against fundamental deterioration but I can't identify the sympathy source cleanly enough to call it OVERDONE.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.thesis: BMI isn't a semi or defense name, so the sector-rotation narrative doesn't apply cleanly — it's a scientific instruments / water utilities adjacent business. Volume is below average (0.84x) yet five-day move is +16%, suggesting persistent accumulation without a single news catalyst. Could be a sell-side note, a peer-derived read-through (Xylem, Roper), or M&A speculation — but with no concrete evidence, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.
entries.what_kills: If the 5-day move is purely momentum/short-covering with no fundamental catalyst, a quick fade is plausible — but I cannot assert that directionally without more data.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible REGN/RGEN ticker confusion unwind given REGN FDA news, but unconfirmed
entries.thesis: RGEN -6.8% on 1.57x volume with XBI +1.9% is a meaningful divergence. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings. The Regeneron FDA PreCheck headline is for REGN (different company) — possible ticker confusion unwind if traders bought RGEN by mistake earlier and are now reversing, but I cannot confirm that mechanism from available data. Without a clear catalyst I can't articulate either side at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If a customer-loss, guidance cut, or competitor (Danaher/Cytiva) win announcement is found, the move is justified and RATIONAL.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible continuation of tariff/import-cost concern, but no specific catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: FIVE -5.4% on average volume with XLY +2.4% is a sharp relative-strength break (~8% underperformance). Five-day is -7.9%, so this is a continuation of weakness, not a one-day surprise. No 8-K, no earnings imminent. Possible tariff/import-cost narrative since FIVE is heavily China-sourced and a risk-on tape might be lifting names with better domestic exposure while leaving import-dependent names behind — but I can't anchor that at conf 3+ without a specific headline.
entries.what_kills: If DLTR/DG/OLLI are up with sector, FIVE-specific issue (channel check, mgmt commentary) is implied; if they're all down, it's a discount-retail narrative.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no detectable catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: JBLU -6.2% is a notable divergence on a risk-on day with oil weakening (which should help airlines) and XLI +0.86%. Volume below average (0.9x), suggesting this isn't capitulation but possibly a forced seller or a single broker action. No 8-K, no earnings near. JBLU is a low-priced, high-short-interest, balance-sheet-stressed name where moves of this size are routine and often technical. Can't articulate either direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If peer airlines (LUV, AAL, UAL, ALK) also down 3-5%, sector narrative; if JBLU alone, idiosyncratic (Spirit-related credit, route news, etc.).

entries.name: Manhattan Associates
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no identified news.
entries.thesis: MANH popping strongly while XLK is down nearly 2% is highly unusual for a quality SaaS name — the relative move is the signal, but with no 8-K, no earnings, and no public news identified, I cannot determine whether this is a takeout rumor, analyst upgrade, customer win, or other. The magnitude warrants attention but I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses on Monday with no news identified, suggesting it was a technical or quant-flow artifact.
